items.title: Resigning mayor makes plea for 'kindness', 'respect' from councillors

children.content: A greater Hobart mayor will resign after serving in a public role for three decades, saying 'keyboard warriors' and a 'toxic culture' from councillors prompted her decision.

image.altText: Four mayors gather for a press conference

items.title: Embattled salmon industry launches advertising blitz amid calls to shut down

children.content: A new advertising campaign featuring the stories of workers adjacent to Tasmania's fish farming industry is launched amid calls for the sector to be shut down.

image.altText: Two women stand side by side.

items.title: 'They deserve death': Officer used dispatch system to monitor police wife

children.content: Text messages read out in court tell how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

gallery.altText: A text message that says: "Those people on your shift are fucking dead."
A man seen in side profile view.

thumbnail.altText: A text message that says: "Those people on your shift are fucking dead."
A man seen in side profile view.
The front entrance of a red brick building with a sign that reads 'city police station'

items.title: How to manage grief and trauma during painful anniversaries

children.content: Next week marks 30 years since the shooting at Port Arthur where 35 people lost their lives. How can you support loved ones when anniversaries bring back painful memories?

image.altText: Port Arthur memorial pool

items.title: Liberty jobs secure for now, but what happens when the money runs out?

children.content: Three weeks of wages have been guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers, but with no buyer locked in, questions remain about what happens next.

image.altText: men and women in orange high vis outside a blue logo and a rusted sculpture

items.title: Court shown Rebel Wilson text exchange about actor's bath incident

children.content: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes in a white shirt and silver jewellery

items.title: What happens after you buy a house?

children.content: You’ve heard it all before: it’s harder to buy a house.
But, if you do manage to get your foot on the property ladder, things are also more difficult afterwards.
The housing crisis has turned into an intergenerational wealth crisis, set to make paying your mortgage in retirement the norm.
So how did we get there? And how do we fix it?

image.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

gallery.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

thumbnail.altText: stylised image of a man and woman walking in front of a suburban home

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms Anzac Day plans

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms he will be attending Anzac Day services tomorrow, saying he intends to pay his respects, one week on from his release on bail.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith is seen during a break the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney

items.title: Pentagon awards $276m AUKUS contract funded by Australia

children.content: The US Department of Defense announces the first major submarine contract to deliver on the AUKUS security deal.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Sniper who killed top bikie in public execution revealed after gag on identity lifted

children.content: The former soldier who publicly assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a cap and white shirt on the right poses in a selfie with a quokka on the left.

items.title: 'Horrendous scream': Camper describes moment a dingo attacked

children.content: Kristen Bennetts says a dingo came dangerously close to her family during a night camping at Karijini National Park. Then, she heard a "horrendous scream".

image.altText: A dingo in green and yellow shrubs looks at the camera with its tongue out.

items.title: Horse trainer's mission to spotlight 'amazing' animal companions used in WWI

children.content: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

articles.title: Which states get a public holiday for Anzac Day?

children.content: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

image.altText: poppies on a cenotaph

articles.title: VC recipient calls for federal government to step up veteran support

children.content: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

image.altText: A man holds a portrait of a young man in military uniform

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again

children.content: Australia's ASX 200 has ended the week on a disappointing note, although the market clawed back most of its earlier losses to finish less than 0.1 per cent down for the day. Meanwhile, oil prices are rising again with Brent crude trading above $US105 a barrel. Follow the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection

children.content: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

image.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

items.title: Tradwives, gangsters and one very special dog: April's 10 best books

children.content: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

articles.title: New Adelaide Writers' Week boss says books, not political views, will inform line-up

children.content: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

items.title: Jane Fonda was in her 40s when she changed the way we exercise

children.content: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

image.altText: Actress Jane Fonda in a yellow exercise outfit stretching with her left foot pointed upward in the air.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

image.altText: A red and black robot on a street with barriers in the background and a ribbon falling to the ground.

items.title: Michael Tilson Thomas, 12-time Grammy-winning conductor, dies aged 81

children.content: The celebrated conductor led the San Francisco Symphony for 25 years, and co-founded the New World Symphony.

items.title: Australia is searching for productivity. Could reducing inequality be the answer?

children.content: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

children.content: The Albanese government is reading the room when it comes to intergenerational equity ahead of the May 12 budget.

items.title: 'This is now terminal': UK braces for prospect of sixth PM in seven years

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

items.title: UK's generational smoking ban applauded in Australia

children.content: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

image.altText: An unidentifiable person's hands holding an unlit cigarette and a lit red lighter.

items.title: Mapping the most critical shipping lanes in the world

children.content: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

items.title: ICAC investigating senior SA police officer for alleged misconduct

children.content: The state's anti-corruption agency says the officer, who cannot be named, is under investigation to determine whether they "committed a breach of discipline contrary to the SA Police Code of Conduct".

items.title: Signal praises Apple over security fix in new iPhone update

children.content: The encrypted messaging app says the update fixes an issue that allowed the FBI to message notification content.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

items.title: 'Nice to be nice': Duo help collapsed runner finish Boston Marathon

children.content: Aaron Beggs, who helped fellow runner Ajay Haridasse across the Boston Marathon finish line, says "the natural instinct was just to go and pick him up".

image.altText: Aaron Beggs and Robson de Oliveria help Ajay Haridasse

items.title: Hundreds of fishing boats sit idle off southern China — but they're not there to fish

children.content: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

gallery.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

thumbnail.altText: A satellite image giving a birdseye view of boats scattered across a bay.

items.title: Dozen more possible human burial sites found at Rottnest Island church

children.content: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

image.altText: A look over rock groynes at boats in a bay at Rottnest with a lighthouse in the background.

items.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine

children.content: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

image.altText: Britain's Prince Harry speaks at the Kyiv Security Forum, he is stood at a lectern behind a microphone

items.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Maduro removal bets

children.content: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

image.altText: Nicolas Maduro being perp walked by multiple heavily armed police.

items.title: Whale rescuers describe skill and courage needed to detangle 40-tonne marine mammal

children.content: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

items.title: Living on idyllic headland poses health problems for mob of kangaroos

children.content: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

image.altText: three kangaroos on a beach in dune grass

items.title: Figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.
